rows.snippet: A "Could not establish connection." Error Occurs When Loading Web Access Scanning. 18 years ago com/kb/1011873/a-quot-could-not-establish-connection-quot-error-occurs-when-loading-web-access-scanning- Web Access 7.2, Web Access 7.2.1, Scanning 7.2, Scanning<... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011873/a-could-not-establish-connection- ...
rows.title: A "Could not establish connection." Error Occurs When Loading Web Access Scanning.
rows.detailTitle: A "Could not establish connection." Error Occurs When Loading Web Access Scanning.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "Could not establish connection." Error Occurs When Loading Web Access Scanning. Product: Web Access 7.2, Web Access 7.2.1, Scanning 7.2, Scanning 7.2.1 KB: 1011873 Published: August 27, 2007 Last Updated: August 27, 2007 Summary When your Web Access virtual directory is configured to use integrated Windows authentication, if you attempt to load Web Access Scanning from a computer that is not on the same network, you may receive the following error message: Could not establish connection. Reason: An error occurred while sending a request to the server. Reason: Unknown IIS error 401[16005]. Cause When your Web Access virtual directory is configured to use integrated Windows authentication, you will be prompted to manually provide Windows credentials when you attempt to log in to Web Access from a computer that is not on the same domain. However, this feature is not available in Web Access Scanning. When you attempt to load Web Access Scanning from outside the domain, it does not prompt you to manually specify authentication information and the log in process fails. Workaround This issue does not occur when accessing Web Access and Web Access Scanning from within the network. If you must access Web Access Scanning from off the network, you can create another IIS virtual directory that is configured for anonymous access. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailTitle: A "The operation timed out." Error Occurs When Scanning Documents Using Web Access.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "The operation timed out." Error Occurs When Scanning Documents Using Web Access. Product: Web Access 7.1 KB: 1011204 Published: June 5, 2006 Last Updated: June 23, 2006 Summary When scanning documents through Web Access, you may receive an MFC/OLE FileUploader error with the following error message: The operation timed out. Cause Web Access Scanning only supports using port 80 for communication. If your Web Access website is configured to use a different TCP port, you will encounter this error. Workaround Configure the Web Access website to use TCP port 80. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Laserfiche 9.2.1 Service Pack 1. 3 years ago Engine, Audit Trail, Laserfiche Client, the Administration Console, Snapshot, and Scanning. See the following lists of changes. Laserfiche Server Search performance has been improved for certai... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013659/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-9- ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche 9.2.1 Service Pack 1.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche 9.2.1 Service Pack 1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche 9.2.1 Service Pack 1. Product: Laserfiche 9.2.1 KB: 1013659 Published: August 6, 2015 Last Updated: February 21, 2023 Summary This article details Service Pack 1 for Laserfiche 9.2.1. Service Pack 1 includes updates for Laserfiche Server, the Full-Text Search Engine, Audit Trail, Laserfiche Client, the Administration Console, Snapshot, and Scanning. See the following lists of changes. Laserfiche Server Search performance has been improved for certain types of AND (&) searches. The optimization only takes effect when the Resolve shortcuts option is not enabled. (125703) Laserfiche Server could become unstable when Laserfiche Client 7.2.1 attempts to connect to a repository. This issue is now resolved. (126716) You no longer receive a "Could not export briefcase. (Unknown internal error. [9036])" error when exporting a page that contains a redaction that covers a majority of the page. (126861) Laserfiche Server could become unstable when Web Access attempts to connect and the client's locale is using a country code that is longer than 2 characters. This issue is now resolved. (127107) The Laserfiche Repository performance counters now properly show the list of available repositories in Performance Monitor. (127155) Laserfiche Server is now better optimized when closing idle connections to the SQL database. (127182, 129322) Laserfiche Server no longer becomes unstable when processing a field search that contains a large number of field values. (128164) Text field search performance is improved when using Oracle 11g. (128714) In certain situations, volume import could create duplicate date and time field definitions. This issue is now resolved. (128829) Search performance is improved when using the Document/Folder Name type search in the Laserfiche Client to do a Document search. (130298) The server update includes the following files: lfs.exe (9.2.1.635) xmlsec.dll (1.2.19.3) FTSKernel.dll (9.2.1.635) Audit Trail The Audit Trail Reporting site now correctly loads audit logs stored in a folder not managed by the Laserfiche Server. (127891) Audit reports pulling from a Microsoft SQL Server 2012 database did not automatically update with new data without either restarting the Audit Reporting service or first changing the date range on the report. This issue is now resolved. (125202) Audit Trail Reporting could fail to create reporting databases on Oracle. This issue is now resolved. (127958) The update includes the following files: AuditDBService.exe (9.2.1.635) AuditRemoting.dll (9.2.1.635) ora-audit.sql WebAuditReport.dll (9.2.1.635) WebAuditConfig.dll (9.2.1.635) LFSO92.dll (9.2.1.568) Interop.LFSO92Lib.dll (9.2.0.0) Laserfiche Client Laserfiche Client no longer displays a "The specified attachment was not selected for separation. [3505]" error when importing an email with attachments using the option to file attachments separately and there is an attachment without a name. Laserfiche now automatically names such attachments Untitled Attachment. (129490) User accounts without the Process feature right can no longer generate Laserfiche image pages for PDF files on import. (127403) There was a potential stability issue when opening a large number of documents in a single Laserfiche Client instance. This issue is now resolved. (128897) The import process now correctly respects the name conflict options when the Autoname option has a Default Document Name with a token and the Importing files option is enabled. (129003) Certain types of PDF files could cause the Generate Pages option to display an "Index was out of range. Must be non-negative and less than the size of the collection." error when the "Preserve PDF annotations on Laserfiche pages" option is enabled. This issue is now resolved. (128953) 1013655 The Laserfiche Client 9.2 Metadata Dialog Box May Be Unable To Properly Display Drop-Down List Items With Certain Versions Of Internet Explorer 11. (125682) The Laserfiche Client may not display certain dynamic fields in the Search Pane when logged in to multiple repositories. This issue affects dynamic fields in templates that that have the same ID as another template in the second repository. (130319) The update includes the following files: lf.exe (9.2.1.635) LFSO92.dll (9.2.1.568) Interop.LFSO92Lib.dll (9.2.0.0) PDFImporter92.dll (9.2.1.619) LFCommonDialogs92.dll (9.2.1.619) LFMetadata92.dll (9.2.1.619) LFDocInterfaces92.dll (9.2.1.619) LFSOFolderBrowser92.dll (9.2.1.578) Laserfiche Administration Console When viewing trustee attributes, the Export option is now available for account names that contain characters that are invalid for Windows file names.(126349) The update includes the following files: lfAdmin.dll (9.2.1.635) Snapshot Snapshot no longer tries to use port 80 to retrieve a list of repositories from Laserfiche Server when SSL is enabled. (127602) Snapshot Configuration now allows you to manually type in Windows credentials with Wi

rows.snippet: A "2106: WebLink Error." Error Occurs When Clicking Browse in WebLink. 22 years ago A "2106: WebLink Error." Error Occurs When Clicking Browse in WebLink. June 28, 2004 | KB: 1000302 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000302/a-quot-2106-weblink-error-qu... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000302/a-2106-weblink-error-error ...
rows.title: A "2106: WebLink Error." Error Occurs When Clicking Browse in WebLink.
rows.detailTitle: A "2106: WebLink Error." Error Occurs When Clicking Browse in WebLink.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "2106: WebLink Error." Error Occurs When Clicking Browse in WebLink. Product: WebLink 6.x KB: 1000302 Published: June 28, 2004 Last Updated: June 28, 2004 Summary When you click the Browse option, you may receive the following error message: 2106: WebLink Error Cause A possible cause for this error is a timeout issue between the WebLink Server and the LaserFiche Server. Resolution Edit the LFWEBLINK.ini file to make sure that the WebLink Server is looking for the correct LaserFiche Server. To update WebLink settings: Open the LFWeblink.ini file. The file can be found in your Windows installation folder. By default, the folder is C:\Windows. Add these lines under the [Database] section: Server=ComputerName\LASERFICHE (or IP Address of LaserFiche Server) Note: If you are using LaserFiche United (Standard\Enterprise), then you should only specify the name of the computer where the SQL server resides. Save the .ini file. Restart the WebLink service for the changes to take effect. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "500 - Internal server error." Error Occurs When Printing to PDF with WebLink 8. 16 years ago An "500 - Internal server error." Error Occurs When Printing to PDF with WebLink 8. November 2, 2009 | KB: 1012372 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012372/an-quot-500-interna... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012372/an-500-internal-server-error-e ...
rows.title: An "500 - Internal server error." Error Occurs When Printing to PDF with WebLink 8.
rows.detailTitle: An "500 - Internal server error." Error Occurs When Printing to PDF with WebLink 8.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in An "500 - Internal server error." Error Occurs When Printing to PDF with WebLink 8. Product: WebLink 8 KB: 1012372 Published: November 2, 2009 Last Updated: November 2, 2009 Summary When you click the Print PDF button in WebLink's document viewer and WebLink 8 is installed on a 64-bit operating system, you may receive the following error message: 500 - Internal server error. There is a problem with the resource you are looking for, and it cannot be displayed. Cause There may be an invalid .PDF handler mapping. This issue can occur if WebLink is not running under the default Web site and the 64-bit version of .NET Framework is not installed on the Web server. When you create a new Web site in IIS on a 64-bit operating system and create a new virtual directory for WebLink, the executable path of the handler mapping for PDF files may point to the default installation path for the 64-bit version of .NET Framework even though the 64-bit version of .NET Framework is not installed. Workaround 1 Install the 64-bit version of the .NET Framework 2.0 redistributable. You can download the installation package from the Microsoft Web site. Workaround 2 If you do not wish to install the 64-bit version of .NET Framework 2.0, modify the .PDF handler mapping to point to the valid 32-bit installation path for .NET Framework. To modify the handler mapping Load the Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager. Expand the tree to the WebLink 8 virtual directory under your custom Web site. In IIS 5 and 6, view the properties of the virtual directory and on the Virtual Directory tab, click the Configuration button. In IIS 7, double-click the Handler Mapping item. Make sure there is only one item for the .pdf extension and that the executable path for the mapping points to "C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework\v2.0.50727\aspnet_isapi.dll." 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "WebLink requires IIS 5.0 or greater." Error Occurs When Installing WebLink 7. 20 years ago A "WebLink requires IIS 5.0 or greater." Error Occurs When Installing WebLink 7. November 16, 2005 | KB: 1011058 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011058/a-quot-webl... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011058/a-weblink-requires-iis-5-0 ...
rows.title: A "WebLink requires IIS 5.0 or greater." Error Occurs When Installing WebLink 7.
rows.detailTitle: A "WebLink requires IIS 5.0 or greater." Error Occurs When Installing WebLink 7.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "WebLink requires IIS 5.0 or greater." Error Occurs When Installing WebLink 7. Product: WebLink 7 KB: 1011058 Published: November 16, 2005 Last Updated: November 16, 2005 Summary When installing WebLink 7, you may receive the following error message: WebLink requires IIS 5.0 or greater. Cause The WebLink installation cannot verify that IIS is installed on the current computer. The WebLink installation checks for the existence of the local path specified for the IIS default web site. If the default directory has been moved, WebLink will be unable to locate it, and the error message will be received. Resolution Verify that the path specified in the Local Path option for the Default Directory in IIS. To specify the local path of the IIS default web site From the Start menu, select Control Panel. Select Administrative Tools. Select Internet Information Services. Expand the server. Expand Web Sites. Select Default Web Site. From the Action menu, select Properties. Select the Home Directory tab. Take note of the path specified in the Local Path option. If the specified path does not exist, perform one of the following: Locate where the folder was moved to and move it back to match the specified path. Change the path to match the actual location of the folder. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

(Exception from HRESULT: 0x80070005 (E_ACCESSDENIED))" Error Occurs When Opening Documents in WebLink. 17 years ago access-is-denied-exception-from-hresult-0x80070005-e_accessdenied-quot-error-occurs-when-opening-documents-in-weblink- WebLink 8 Summary When you open a document through WebLink 8... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012243/an-access-is-denied-exception-fro ...
rows.title: An "Access is denied. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80070005 (E_ACCESSDENIED))" Error Occurs When Opening Documents in WebLink.
rows.detailTitle: An "Access is denied. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80070005 (E_ACCESSDENIED))" Error Occurs When Opening Documents in WebLink.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Access is denied. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80070005 (E_ACCESSDENIED))" Error Occurs When Opening Documents in WebLink. Product: WebLink 8 KB: 1012243 Published: June 8, 2009 Last Updated: June 8, 2009 Summary When you open a document through WebLink 8, you may receive the following error message: Access is denied. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80070005 (E_ACCESSDENIED)) Cause The user account used to authenticate to IIS may not have sufficient Windows permissions to one or more of the following Windows folders: The WebLink temporary image cache and PDF cache folders: These folder locations is customizable through the WebLink 8 Administrator's Utility. By default, they are located at "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink 8\tempDirectory" and "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink 8\tempPDFDirectory." The WebLink "Web Files" folder: This folder is located in your WebLink 8 installation folder. By default, it is located at "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink 8\Web Files." The Windows TEMP system environment variable: By default, the variable is usually configured to "C:\Windows\Temp." The WebLink log files folder: WebLink may write logging information to the "C:\Temp\WebLink Log" folder. Resolution If WebLink is configured for anonymous access, make sure that the specified Windows user account can read and write to the specified folders. If WebLink is configured for Windows authentication, make sure that all necessary domain users can access the folders. Related Links 1012586 Modifying the WebLink 8 Log File Path. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Known Issues with WebLink 7.0.5 19 years ago known issues with WebLink 7.0.5. More Information Laserfiche WebLink Known Issues 1011926 An "Exception from HResult: 0x80040BBD" Error Occurs When Viewing Large Images.... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011256/known-issues-with-weblink- ...
rows.title: Known Issues with WebLink 7.0.5
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Known Issues with WebLink 7.0.5 Product: WebLink 7.0.5 KB: 1011256 Published: August 21, 2006 Last Updated: August 21, 2006 Summary This article documents known issues with WebLink 7.0.5. More Information Laserfiche WebLink Known Issues 1011926 An "Exception from HResult: 0x80040BBD" Error Occurs When Viewing Large Images. (36033) 1011907 Color or Grayscale Images Do Not Display Clearly When Viewed at Less Than 100% Magnification. 1011871 A "CS0016: Could not write to output file." Error Occurs When Loading WebLink. 1011835 Long Template Field Data May Not Display Correctly in Netscape and Mozilla-based Browsers. (27768) 1011724 An "An error occurred. Not all of the features were successfully installed." Error Occurs When Installing the ASP.NET feature in IIS 7.0. 1011722 An "Error 80040154" Occurs When Loading WebLink on Windows Server 2003 x64. 1011671 The WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility May Change the Fields in a Repository Configuration to all Upper Case Characters. (27968) 1011648 The Folder Browser Page and Search Results Page Did Not Support the Target Attribute. Product WebLink 7. (27929) 1011642 Internet Standards Do Not Fully Support Having an Underscore Character In a Server Hostname. 1011639 A "Parameter is incorrect." Error Occurs When Opening Documents When WebLink 7.0.5 is Configured to use the Firewall Compatibility Component. (27840) 1011637 A "The directory ''Weblink7'' could not be found in the default web site." Error Occurs When Loading the Laserfiche WebLink 7 Administrator's Utility. 1011251 The Firewall Compatibility Component May Stop Functioning After You Upgrade to Laserfiche 7.2. 1000954 Content Displayed in a WebLink Page Appears as Pure Text. WebLink 7.0.5 does not allow you to change your password from the Login page. This can cause issues if your user account is configured such that you are required to change your password at the next login. If you attempt to log in through WebLink, you will receive a "This password has expired and must be changed." error message. However, you will not have the option of changing your password from that page. A Laserfiche Administrator must clear that setting on your account or you must first log in with that user account using the Laserfiche Windows client. (27010) There is no option to view the tags associated with a folder. (27224) Sticky notes are always displayed using the yellow sticky note image. (27030) The folders pane on the left of the folder browser window does not support displaying nested folders more than 32 folders deep. (20762) WebLink may encounter issues when generating a color PDF for e-sized images. ASP.NET stops any process serving web requests if the process takes up too much memory. For color e-sized images, the PDF generation process may exceed this memory limit. As a workaround, download the e-sized image as a black and white PDF. (20299) If you use the web browser's Back button after adding or removing display columns, WebLink will not automatically refresh the display. (19995) Searches cannot be cancelled. (19385) Advanced search syntax is not supported when using basic text search. (18979) The Fit to Height option does not function on Safari web browsers. (18548) This version of Laserfiche WebLink does not support Laserfiche COLDLink. If you would like to use Laserfiche COLDLink, then you should use Laserfiche WebLink version 6.1. If your HTTP (web) Server currently supports multiple port assignments, you must include the correct port assignment in the WebLink URL. For example, http://YourServerName:81 /WebLink/. Related Links Please refer to the following article to learn more about Laserfiche WebLink 7.0.5. 1011255 List of Changes for WebLink 7.0.5. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "Server Application Unavailable" Error Occurs When Accessing WebLink. 20 years ago laserfiche.com/kb/1011126/a-quot-server-application-unavailable-quot-error-occurs-when-accessing-weblink- WebLink 7 Summary When trying to access the WebLink site, you may receive... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011126/a-server-application-unavailable- ...
rows.title: A "Server Application Unavailable" Error Occurs When Accessing WebLink.
rows.detailTitle: A "Server Application Unavailable" Error Occurs When Accessing WebLink.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "Server Application Unavailable" Error Occurs When Accessing WebLink. Product: WebLink 7 KB: 1011126 Published: February 20, 2006 Last Updated: February 20, 2006 Summary When trying to access the WebLink site, you may receive one of the following error messages: Server Application Unavailable Server cannot access application directory 'C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink 7\Web Files\'. The directory does not exist or is not accessible because of security settings. The Event Viewer may contain errors involving the ASP.NET process identity not having sufficient permissions. Cause In IIS 5, ASP.NET web application code runs in a worker process using a local machine account named ASPNET. The ASPNET account must be able to read the Web Files subfolder of your WebLink 7 installation folder. Resolution Grant the ASPNET local machine account read access to the Web Files subfolder located in your WebLink 7 installation folder. To change the security for the Web Files folder Using Windows Explorer, browse to the WebLink 7 installation folder. Select the Web Files subfolder. From the File menu, click Properties. Click the Security tab. Add the ASPNET local machine account and allow Read. Click OK to save your changes and close the dialog box. Related Links For information on the Windows permissions needed by standard user accounts logging in to WebLink, please see the following Knowledge Base article: 1000943 : Minimum Set of Windows Permissions Needed to Use WebLink. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1 5 years ago Laserfiche App Summary This article lists the changes for Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1. More Information Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1 (KB 1013727) includes the following ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013727/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1 Product: Laserfiche App KB: 1013727 Published: February 11, 2016 Last Updated: October 13, 2020 Summary This article lists the changes for Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1. More Information Laserfiche Mobile Server 10 Update 1 (KB 1013727) includes the following updates: The app no longer crashes when adding a list field containing lots of list items to an entry. (139808) The app no longer returns an "Access Denied" error when access rights are configured in a specific way. (139205) Effective feature rights are now reflected on the Laserfiche Mobile Server. (138054) Newly uploaded electronic files (PDF, Excel, etc.) can now be saved successfully without displaying an "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error. (140089) The Licensing Site field now reflects the actual site name instead of the database name and correctly populates the Organization drop-down menu. (137913) Users with repository accounts can now sign in to Laserfiche Mobile if a domain name has been configured on the Laserfiche Mobile Configuration page. (137879) Automatic sign-in will now work when a domain name is configured on the Laserfiche Mobile Configuration page. (137710) An appropriate error is now returned when a Forms server is configured incorrectly on the Configuration Page. (137367) Imaged documents opened from search results are no longer distorted. (137555) Imaged documents saved as offline copies are no longer distorted. (137514) The update includes the following files: GlobalAssemblyCacheNet4_100 (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.PdfServices.dll (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.PdfServices.Compression.dll (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.I18n.dll (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.HttpClient.dll (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.RepositoryAccess.dll (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.DocumentServices.dll (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.TextProviderClient.dll (10.0.0.402) Laserfiche.RepositoryAccess.resources.dll under (ar, es, fr, it, ko, pt-BR, tr, vi, zh-Hans, zh-Hant) (10.0.0.402) WebAccessMobileCommonConfig.dll (10.0.0.402) WebAccessMobileImaging.dll (10.0.0.402) Web.Util.dll (10.0.0.402) WebAccessMobile.dll (10.0.0.402) WebAccessMobileCommon.dll (10.0.0.402) LfAuthenticationLib.dll (10.0.0.402) LfOCRLib.dll (10.0.0.402) LicenseManagerObjects.dll (10.0.0.402) WebAccessMobileResources.resources.dll (10.0.0.402) Resolution To update the Laserfiche Mobile Server 10: Click one of the following links to download either the 32-bit or 64-bit version of the update. Each zip file contains a single patch file (.msp). Extract the contents of the zip file to a temporary location on the machine where the Laserfiche Mobile Server is installed. Double-click the .msp file to update the Laserfiche Mobile Server. KB1013727_x86.zip KB1013727_x64.zip Related Links Release Notes for Laserfiche Mobile 10 List of Changes for Laserfiche Mobile 10 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

It does not have access to sessions on the Quick Fields Server ... potential insta... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014431/release-notes-for-laserfiche-quic ...
rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche Quick Fields 11 Update 3
rows.detailTitle: Release Notes for Laserfiche Quick Fields 11 Update 3
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche Quick Fields 11 Update 3 Product: Quick Fields KB: 1014431 Published: March 22, 2023 Last Updated: July 6, 2023 Summary Quick Fields 11 Update 3 updates to Quick Fields, Quick Fields Server, Quick Fields Agent, and Quick Fields Scanning. For a more detailed list of changes, see Knowledge Base article 1014427 List of Changes for Laserfiche Quick Fields 11 Update 3. This article covers: Installation information System requirements Software requirements Known issues Installation Information The Quick Fields 11 Update 3 installation will upgrade any existing Quick Fields 11 installations. System Requirements Quick Fields, Quick Fields Scanning, and Quick Fields Agent, and the Quick Fields Server (Administration Console) Requirements CPU: 2.4 GHz processor Memory: 2 GB RAM Operating system: Windows Server 2012 R2, Windows 10, Windows Server 2016, Windows Server 2019, Windows 11, Windows Server 2022 Software Requirements Quick Fields, Quick Fields Scanning, and Quick Fields Agent Windows Components Microsoft .NET Framework 4.8 Note: Quick Fields performs best with Laserfiche Server 11 and later. Quick Fields 11 fully supports the Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS). Laserfiche Components Laserfiche Server 10.4 and later Quick Fields Server 10.3 and later Quick Fields Server (Administration Console) Windows Components Microsoft .NET Framework 4.8 Internet Information Services (IIS) and the following components: IIS Static Content ASP.NET 4.8 Microsoft SQL Server 2014 SP3, Microsoft SQL Server 2016, Microsoft SQL Server 2017, Microsoft SQL Server 2019 or Microsoft SQL Server 2022 Internet Browsers You will need an Internet browser to access the Quick Fields Administration Console. The following browsers are supported: Google Chrome (latest version) Mozilla Firefox (latest version) Microsoft Edge (latest version) Known Issues See the following list of known issues in Quick Fields 11 Update 3 Quick Fields If you are using Windows 8 or 10, scanning from a mapped network drive may not work. Scan using the full path instead of the drive letter, or follow these steps to modify the registry. (141757) SingleLine mode is not enabled when applying a pattern to a token. Insert (?s) at the beginning of the pattern to turn SingleLine mode on. Insert (?-s) to turn SingleLine mode off. (132600) Sessions created in Quick Fields 10 cannot be opened successfully in Quick Fields 9. (127227) Stamps on documents retrieved using Laserfiche Capture Engine are not displayed on the image in Quick Fields. (144944) Changes to template or field definitions must be republished to the Quick Fields Server for them to be available in Quick Fields Scanning. (143603) An Unhandled Exception error occurs when configuring "Send Documents" schedule in the Session Wizard (Standard/Full). (146553) Running a 9.1 Barcode process may fail when using Windows 10. Install Quick Fields 10 and uninstall Barcode 9.1 to solve this issue. See KB 101378. (133053) Publishing a name change to a document class does not work. (151092) Uninstalling Quick Fields from Add/Remove Programs does not uninstall the language packs. (151144) Blank independent fields added to a document in Quick Fields are not retained when the document is stored to the repository. (27858) When using Color Removal and displaying each scanned image before processing, the Document Revision pane does not always display the processed image correctly. (44626) ScanConnect can sometimes become unresponsive when repeatedly switching between sessions that use ScanConnect. (50049) If the When a document is selected option is set to Edit the document?s default field in Revision pane options, and the document has no default field, the focus will remain on the document. (53755) In Quick Fields, an Object reference not set error message appears when scanning PDFs with Universal Capture if Include PDF form field values in the text is selected. (68166) In Quick Fields, if you view a document in the Document Revision pane that has the same template as the previous document viewed, multi-value field groups with real-time lookup will drop the tail of instances. (93690) In Quick Fields, a multi-value field value does not update from typing an input while a new value is being created. (90011) In Quick Fields, opening a session when another session is already open slows down multi-value field and multi-value field group performance. (89673) Quick Fields only retrieves the list of user tables in a lookup data source and not all tables and views. (219537) In Quick Fields, tokens that are populated in fields on document storage are marked as invalid before they are stored. (228677) In Quick Fields, tokens that are populated for folder names on document storage are marked as invalid before they are stored. (228676) In Quick Fields, when using a real-time process with the Clear fields when no data is retrieved option selected, the field is not cleared
